name: IOC Lifecycle Management
description: Manages the full lifecycle of Indicators of Compromise by periodically reviewing active indicators, expiring stale ones, and refreshing those still relevant. The playbook queries all active IOCs, checks their age and last-seen dates, re-enriches aging indicators to validate continued relevance, then expires truly stale indicators and refreshes active ones with updated threat data. An audit report summarizing lifecycle actions is generated and distributed for compliance tracking.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: VirusTotal
required_integrations.reason: Re-enrich aging indicators to validate their continued relevance and threat status

required_integrations.name: MISP
required_integrations.reason: Check indicator freshness against MISP events and update sighting data

config.description: Triggers on a daily schedule or on-demand to review and manage the lifecycle of active indicators

data.label: Query Active IOCs and Classify by Age

config.code:
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

now = datetime.utcnow()
active_iocs = inputs.get('active_indicators', [])

stale_threshold_days = 90
refresh_threshold_days = 30

stale = []
refresh_needed = []
current = []

for ioc in active_iocs:
    created = datetime.fromisoformat(ioc.get('created_at', now.isoformat()))
    last_seen = datetime.fromisoformat(ioc.get('last_seen', ioc.get('created_at', now.isoformat())))
    age_days = (now - created).days
    days_since_seen = (now - last_seen).days
    
    ioc['age_days'] = age_days
    ioc['days_since_seen'] = days_since_seen
    
    if days_since_seen > stale_threshold_days:
        stale.append(ioc)
    elif days_since_seen > refresh_threshold_days:
        refresh_needed.append(ioc)
    else:
        current.append(ioc)

result = {
    'total_active': len(active_iocs),
    'stale_count': len(stale),
    'refresh_count': len(refresh_needed),
    'current_count': len(current),
    'stale': stale,
    'refresh_needed': refresh_needed,
    'current': current,
    'review_date': now.isoformat()
}
config.description: Queries all active IOCs and classifies them into stale (>90 days unseen), refresh-needed (>30 days), and current categories
Iterates over aging indicators to re-enrich and validate continued relevance
Re-enriches each aging indicator against VT and MISP to validate whether it remains a relevant threat

config.additional_instructions: Assess the lifecycle status of all reviewed indicators. For stale indicators, confirm they should be expired. For refreshed indicators, determine if they remain relevant threats or should be downgraded. Provide a summary of lifecycle actions recommended.
config.description: AI-powered assessment determining which indicators to expire, refresh, or retain based on enrichment results

config.code:
stale = inputs.get('stale', [])
assessment = inputs.get('assessment', {})

expired_ids = [ioc.get('id') for ioc in stale]
refreshed_ids = assessment.get('refresh_ids', [])
downgraded_ids = assessment.get('downgrade_ids', [])

result = {
    'expired': expired_ids,
    'expired_count': len(expired_ids),
    'refreshed': refreshed_ids,
    'refreshed_count': len(refreshed_ids),
    'downgraded': downgraded_ids,
    'downgraded_count': len(downgraded_ids),
    'actions_summary': f'Expired: {len(expired_ids)}, Refreshed: {len(refreshed_ids)}, Downgraded: {len(downgraded_ids)}'
}
config.description: Applies lifecycle actions: expires stale indicators, refreshes validated ones, and downgrades no-longer-relevant indicators

notify.subject: IOC Lifecycle Audit — {{code_1.outputs.result.review_date}}

config.description: Distributes the IOC lifecycle audit report to the threat intel team for compliance tracking
Archives the lifecycle audit record as a note for compliance and historical reference
